column3:
Multiplier Logic Note

The multiplier rows compare each attribution model against Last Click Sales, which is used as the baseline. The purpose is to understand how much higher or lower each model reports sales compared to Last Click.

Weekly Multiplier vs Last Click

This is calculated as:

Channel Sales ÷ Last Click Sales

4-Week Avg Multiplier

This takes the average of the last 4 weekly multipliers. It helps reduce weekly noise and gives a more reliable trend than looking at one week alone.

Stability

This uses the standard deviation of the last 4 weekly multipliers. A lower stability number means the multiplier is more consistent and predictable. A higher number means the multiplier is more volatile.

Range Flag

The range flag checks the 4-Week Avg Multiplier, not the weekly multiplier. This tells us whether the rolling multiplier is below, within, or above the expected range.

Confidence Level

Confidence is based on both the range flag and stability.

High means the 4-week average is in range and the multiplier is stable.
Medium means the 4-week average is in range, but the multiplier is more volatile.
Low means the 4-week average is outside the expected range.

These calculations are applied only to the weekly columns G:BC. Monthly, quarterly, and annual columns are not included in this weekly multiplier logic.
